title: Greek-Style Hung Curd Bowl with Fruit and Nuts

ingredients.name: hung curd (curd drained in muslin for 2-3 hours or overnight)

subs.name: store-bought thick Greek yogurt — identical result with zero prep time
pumpkin seeds (pepitas) for a nut-free version with similar crunch
date syrup or jaggery dissolved in a splash of warm water — vegan-friendly sweetness

steps.text: To make hung curd: line a bowl with a muslin cloth or a fine strainer, pour in full-fat or double-toned curd and leave in the fridge for at least 2-3 hours (overnight gives a thicker, more yogurt-like consistency). The whey will drain away, leaving a dense, creamy curd with concentrated protein.

Toast the mixed nuts in a dry frying pan on low heat, stirring constantly, for 3-4 minutes until they turn lightly golden and fragrant. Watch carefully — nuts go from golden to burnt in seconds. Remove and let cool briefly before roughly chopping.
Dice the apple into small chunks and slice the banana, if using. Working quickly prevents the apple from browning — a light squeeze of lemon helps if you are prepping ahead.
Spoon the thick hung curd into a wide bowl and smooth the surface with the back of the spoon. Arrange the fruit on top in sections.

Scatter the toasted nuts over the fruit, drizzle honey in a thin stream, dust with cinnamon and sprinkle flax seeds. Eat immediately for the best contrast of textures between the cool creamy curd and the warm crunchy nuts.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: curd → store-bought thick Greek yogurt — identical result with zero prep time; almond → pumpkin seeds (pepitas) for a nut-free version with similar crunch; honey → date syrup or jaggery dissolved in a splash of warm water — vegan-friendly sweetness
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Hung curd is the perfect stand-in for Greek yogurt — simply pour standard dahi into a muslin cloth or fine strainer and refrigerate for 2-3 hours. Full-fat Amul curd or homemade curd works best. Almonds (badam), cashews (kaju) and walnuts (akhrot) are all available at any Indian kirana store; banana and apple are the easiest fruit choices year-round.
